There was a great movement of the populace on the Plaza-Mayor, that forum of the ancient city of kings; artisans were profiting by the coolness to quit their daily labors; they circulated actively among the crowd, crying their various merchandise; the ladies of Lima, carefully enveloped in the mantillas which mask their countenances, with the exception of the right eye, darted stealthy glances on the surrounding masses; they undulated through the groups of smokers, like foam at the will of the waves; other señoras, in ball costume, _coiffed_ only with their abundant hair or some natural flowers, passed in large calêches, throwing on the _caballeros_ nonchalant regards.

In April the streets of New York are still white with snow, yet the latitude of New York is nearly the same as that of the Azores. Gun-cotton, or pyroxyle, is the nitro-cellulose explosive that Verne makes the favoured propellant of his engineers, from the lunar cannon of *[[from-the-earth-to-the-moon|From the Earth to the Moon]]* to the castaways of *[[the-mysterious-island|The Mysterious Island]]*. It is defined as "pyroxyle, that is to say gun-cotton, a substance in which cotton is not indispensable, as the elementary tissue of vegetables may be used" (MI-030-007).
